title: Basic OIDC Authentication
description: Start here. You'll see how a user logs in through an identity provider with SSO — everything else builds on top of this.

title: Authorization Code + PKCE
description: The go-to OAuth flow for SPAs and mobile apps. You'll learn how PKCE stops attackers from hijacking authorization codes — and why every public client needs it.

title: Refresh Token Grant
description: So your access token expired. Now what? Walk through silent renewal with refresh tokens and see how token rotation catches replay attacks before they do damage.

title: Client Credentials (M2M)
description: The most straightforward OAuth flow you'll encounter — no user involved, just one machine talking to another. Perfect for microservices, cron jobs, and CI/CD pipelines.

title: App-to-App: Calendar ↔ Zoom
description: Ever wonder how two apps share your data without sharing your password? Walk through multi-party OAuth and see delegation in action.

title: Device Authorization Grant
description: Think about your smart TV or a CLI tool — no keyboard, no browser. You'll see how the device asks you to approve access from your phone while it patiently polls for an answer.

title: Implicit Grant (Deprecated)
description: The old-school SPA flow that dropped tokens right into the URL fragment. Walk through it to understand exactly why OAuth 2.1 killed it — knowing what went wrong sharpens your security instincts.

title: Password Grant (Deprecated)
description: Your app gets the user's actual password — which defeats the entire point of OAuth. See how this grant accidentally trains users to fall for phishing.

title: API Key Delegation (The Problem)
description: What happens when a user hands their API key to an AI agent? Walk through the security holes that open up and why you'd never want this in production.

title: Agent as OAuth Client
description: A smarter move — the AI agent registers as its own OAuth client. Better than sharing keys, but notice how the identity provider still can't see the full picture.

title: Cross-App Access (ID-JAG)
description: Here's where it all comes together. Identity Assertion Authorization Grant gives the identity provider full visibility into who's accessing what across apps.
